# SBDC Business Specialists Overview

## Resource type
Specialist referral resource.

## Best for
Existing or new SBDC clients who need targeted expert help beyond initial advising.

## Best-fit BOSS lifecycle stages
Formation, Launch, Operations, Growth, Recovery.

## Use when the entrepreneur says
"I need QuickBooks help," "I need HR help," "I need cybersecurity help," "I need marketing/branding/SEO help," "I need compliance or accounting support."

## Resource summary
The Business Specialists page describes no-cost specialized expertise through one-on-one virtual sessions in areas including retail, branding, finances, hospitality, marketing, social media, accounting/bookkeeping/contracts, cybersecurity/business continuity/insurance standards, HR, and marketing/AI productivity.

## What the user can do with this resource
Existing clients contact their SBDC advisor for specialist coordination; new clients first find their local SBDC and complete an initial advising session.

## BOSS routing notes
Specialists should usually be a second-step referral after local SBDC intake unless the user is already a client.

## Related source URLs
- https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Consulting1.asp

## BOSS Library use notes
- Treat this as a resource-routing document, not as legal, tax, lending, certification, or procurement advice.
- Use the linked source URL as the user-facing official link.
- Ask discovery questions before routing when the user is early-stage or unclear.
- For Houston/77084 users, verify county/service-area fit through the official SBDC/SBA locator before giving a final local-office referral.
